description: Incident severity matrix for GrantLayer operational incidents. This matrix is a design-time artifact; no incident response implementation is provided by GL-073.

severityLevels.description: Critical incident requiring immediate response. Complete service outage or severe integrity/security breach.
examples: Complete API outage for all endpoints
Suspected secret exposure or credential compromise
Audit/provenance integrity breach or tamper detection
Data corruption affecting product records with no known good backup
severityLevels.userImpact: All users affected; institutional operations halted; potential compliance or legal exposure.
severityLevels.dataIntegrityRisk: Critical — data may be corrupted, lost, or integrity chain broken.
severityLevels.securityRisk: Critical — secrets may be exposed, unauthorized access may have occurred.

severityLevels.description: Major incident with significant impact. Severe degradation or high-severity security concern.
examples: Severe API degradation with >50% error rate
Authentication system unavailable
Suspected data corruption in product records
Backup failure with no successful recent backup
severityLevels.userImpact: Most users affected; core workflows impaired.
severityLevels.dataIntegrityRisk: High — partial data loss or inconsistency suspected.
severityLevels.securityRisk: High — potential unauthorized access or auth bypass.

severityLevels.description: Significant incident with moderate impact. Partial degradation or operational concern.
examples: Partial feature degradation or intermittent errors
Database connection pool exhaustion or query timeout spikes
Backup completion failure or integrity check failure
Evidence verification failures affecting a subset of records
severityLevels.userImpact: Some users affected; non-core features may be impaired.
severityLevels.dataIntegrityRisk: Medium — localized data inconsistency or verification failure.
severityLevels.securityRisk: Medium — potential configuration drift or localized exposure.

severityLevels.description: Minor incident with limited impact. Non-critical bug or operational inconvenience.
examples: Non-critical bug in a single endpoint
Elevated latency below degradation threshold
Single-tenant demo environment issue
Minor configuration warning without functional impact
severityLevels.userImpact: Few users affected; workaround typically available.
severityLevels.dataIntegrityRisk: Low — no data loss or corruption expected.

severityLevels.description: Low-priority issue with negligible impact. Cosmetic or documentation concern.
examples: Cosmetic UI or dashboard display issue
Documentation gap or outdated runbook reference
Internal tool inconvenience without user impact
Non-urgent hygiene finding from routine review
severityLevels.userImpact: None or negligible; no operational impact.
